list.emptyDescription: Click "Generate a new world" to start.
list.structuredPreview: Contains structured settings — open the workspace for details.
list.confirmDelete: Delete world "{{name}}"? This cannot be undone.

stepOne.noGenres: No genre foundations yet. The world wizard now uses the genre foundation library.
stepOne.hintLibrary: This wizard pulls directly from the genre foundation library and no longer uses the template's built-in type list as the entry.
stepOne.hintFlow: Pick a genre first, then we generate the concept card, properties, and the template shortlist.
stepOne.knowledgeDescription: These act as reference sources — we first extract anchors from the source work, then propose alternate-world directions.
stepOne.preservePlaceholder: e.g. realistic urban backdrop, rental-life texture, adult emotional tension
stepOne.allowedPlaceholder: e.g. city tiers, social rules, faction network, location system
stepOne.forbiddenPlaceholder: e.g. no supernatural shift, no shounen power-up arc, no breaking real-world social logic
stepOne.inspirationPlaceholderRef: Paste excerpts from the source, your summary of its world, or how you read it. Or just use the knowledge docs above.
stepOne.optionsCountHint: Borrowed from the old V2 flow: generate selectable world properties first, then enter the formal creation step.
stepOne.progressHintRef: This step runs in order: tidy reference material, extract source-world anchors, generate alt-world decisions.
stepOne.progressHintFree: This step runs in order: tidy inspiration input, generate the concept card, generate upfront property options.
stepOne.extractedMeta: Auto-chunked: source {{length}} characters, split into {{chunks}} segments.
stepOne.analyzeError: Could not parse the world analysis result.
stepOne.missingConcept: The world analysis result is missing a concept card.

stepTwo.propertiesHint: We've pre-checked the suggested ones. Usually you only need to uncheck what you don't want, or add a one-line preference.

stepThree.hint: These are the core rules we tidied up first. Edit directly, or leave them and refine in the editor later.
stepThree.createDraftFailed: Failed to create the world draft.

adapt_world.label: Alt-world adaptation based on source
adapt_world.description: Keep the source world's foundation; then decide which rules, factions and locations may be altered.

extract_base.label: Extract the source world's base
extract_base.description: Lock in the source world's skeleton first; later expansion stays close to source facts.

tone_rebuild.label: Only borrow the source's tone & structure to rebuild
tone_rebuild.description: Keep the atmosphere, relationship structure and life-feel, but allow heavy rebuilding of the world facts.

library.title: Pre-pull from asset library
library.hint: V2 used to let you pull from the property library before formal generation. We keep that idea, wired into the current blueprint.
library.searchPlaceholder: Search the asset library by name or keyword
library.empty: No matching asset entries right now.

property.empty: No usable directions yet. The previous analysis probably failed — go back to Step 1 and regenerate.
property.detailPlaceholder: Optional: add your preference — what to keep, amplify, or limit.
property.libraryReason: Reusable setting from the asset library.

referenceSeed.empty: We couldn't stably extract organizations, locations or rules from the reference this time. The rest will keep generating along your adaptation direction.
referenceSeed.hint: We've pulled a batch of ready-made settings from the reference work and pre-checked them. Keeping them cuts down a lot of manual entry later.
referenceSeed.defaultDetail: Identified as a setting that can be reused directly from the source.

rules.title: Source rules
rules.description: How this world runs by default — auto-generation later will reference these underlying rules.

factions.title: Faction stances
factions.description: Who's on which side, believes what, wants to push what. Good for preserving the source's big directions.

forces.title: Organizations & forces
forces.description: Concrete companies, departments, gangs and people-networks that can step onto the stage immediately.

locations.title: Locations & scenes
locations.description: Cities, neighborhoods, offices, residences that can be used right away.

workspace.confirmDelete: Delete world "{{name}}"? This cannot be undone.

axiomsCard.title: Core rules (axioms)
axiomsCard.hint: Think of these as "the underlying rules this world cannot casually break". Subsequent auto-generation and consistency checks will reference this list.

layersTab.title: Layered build
layersTab.hintInitial: First-time generation builds the 6 layers in parallel.
layersTab.hintReady: Initial generation complete — per-layer AI rewrite is available.

deepeningTab.title: Q&A deepening
deepeningTab.quickOptionsLabel: Quick options (returned by the model — one-click fill)
deepeningTab.noQuickOptions: No quick options returned for this question — please answer manually.

consistencyTab.title: Consistency check
consistencyTab.emptyIssues: No consistency issues yet. Run a check and results will appear here.

summaryFallback.pass: Consistency check passed — no obvious hard conflicts.

messages.THEMATIC_INCOHERENCE: Retrieved supplementary content introduced a thematic framework that doesn't match the core setting.
messages.REDUNDANT_AXIOM_APPLICATION: The supplement just restates existing axioms — no new effective constraint added.
messages.AXIOM_VIOLATION: The world name or core concept conflicts with established axioms or background.
messages.GENRE_MISMATCH: Genre signals don't match the current world's constraints.
messages.AXIOM_MAGIC_CONFLICT: World axiom conflicts with the power-system setup.
messages.TECH_ERA_MISMATCH: Tech-era feel is mixed without enough explanation.
messages.CONFLICT_WEAK: Core-conflict info is too thin — not enough to support the story.
messages.BASELINE_PASS: No obvious hard conflicts at the rule level.

details.THEMATIC_INCOHERENCE: The auxiliary context introduced a theme not clearly established in the original setting — likely to drift the world's main axis.
details.REDUNDANT_AXIOM_APPLICATION: The current supplement mostly repeats existing rules — trim the redundancy and keep only the truly new constraints.
details.AXIOM_VIOLATION: The current name, genre promise or core concept doesn't match the world's underlying rules — unify the main setting.
details.GENRE_MISMATCH: Current names or keywords signal a different genre expectation than the style and rules of this world.
details.AXIOM_MAGIC_CONFLICT: You restricted supernatural/magic in the world axioms, but the power system or related text reintroduces it.
details.TECH_ERA_MISMATCH: Current tech description mixes elements from different eras without explaining origin, limits or transition logic.
details.CONFLICT_WEAK: Add the two sides of the conflict, triggering events, escalation path and failure cost so the main world tension is clearer.
details.fallback: The system flagged an issue related to {{field}}. Cross-check this risk against the current world setting.

profile.title: World profile
profile.themesPlaceholder: Theme keywords, separated by commas or 、

factions.title: Factions & forces
factions.noteLine1: Faction = abstract stance, line or position; Force = concrete organization, circle, network or institution.
factions.noteLine2: World-level default rules like "social pressure mechanism", "industry operating rules", "social network rules" should go to the rule center, not into a faction card.
factions.factionHint: A faction card describes an abstract stance, not a concrete company, department or social network.
factions.factionNamePlaceholder: Faction name, e.g. "In-system stability faction", "Market-profit faction", "Network-pragmatist faction"
factions.factionGoalsPlaceholder: Long-term goals, separated by commas or 、
factions.factionMethodsPlaceholder: Common methods, separated by commas or 、
factions.factionRepresentativePlaceholder: Representative force IDs, separated by commas or 、
factions.forceHint: A force card describes a concrete organization or circle that can apply pressure, occupy locations and join the relationship network.
factions.forceNamePlaceholder: Force name, e.g. "Ad agency management", "Real-estate broker chain", "Local business network"
factions.forceTypePlaceholder: Force type, e.g. company / department / broker network / business circle
factions.forceSummaryPlaceholder: Force summary / outward identity / role in the world
factions.forceBasePlaceholder: Power base / resource source / control levers
factions.forceObjectivePlaceholder: Current objective / what it wants to push right now
factions.forcePressurePlaceholder: Pressure approach / source / how it pushes characters
factions.forceNarrativePlaceholder: Narrative role, e.g. oppressor / tempter / gatekeeper / buffer

binding.title: Binding suggestions
binding.hint: Read-only at this stage — not wired into novel binding yet.

assetsTab.title: Asset library + snapshots + import / export
assetsTab.publishHint: Publish the current setting to the asset library
assetsTab.publishDescriptionPlaceholder: Optional description (defaults to current layer content if blank)
